tools.name: list_routes
tools.description: List all active airport-transfer routes with their slug, endpoints, distance, travel time, available directions, and starting price. Use this first to discover what routes exist.

tools.name: get_pricing
tools.description: Get fixed per-vehicle prices for a route, or for all routes if no routeSlug is given. Prices are fixed (no surge, no metered fares), in EUR and RSD. Each vehicle tier lists max passengers and luggage.

tools.name: estimate_price
tools.description: Estimate the price for a trip. Given a route and passenger count (and optional luggage count), returns the cheapest vehicle that fits the group, plus all fitting options. Use this to answer 'how much for N people from X to the airport?'.

tools.name: get_service_info
tools.description: Get business info: service area, accepted payment methods, supported languages, minimum booking lead time, contact details, and how to book (including the booking URL template).

tools.name: create_booking_request
tools.description: Start a provisional airport-transfer booking. This does NOT finalize a booking — it emails the passenger a one-tap confirmation link, and the ride is only booked once they click it. Payment is to the driver (cash or card); no online payment is needed. Call estimate_price first to confirm the vehicle

tools.name: get_booking_request_status
tools.description: Check whether a booking request (from create_booking_request) has been confirmed by the passenger yet. Returns 'pending', 'confirmed' (with the booking number), or 'expired'.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_service_info, create_booking_request · Private data: get_service_info · Public sink: create_booking_request.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_service_info, create_booking_request · Private data: get_service_info · Public sink: create_booking_request

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
